title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot confronts Alistair Brent, revealing that Brent’s status as a wealthy banker is built upon bigamy. Poirot explains that when Brent married Rebecca Arnold, he was already married to a woman living as Mrs. Albert Chatterton in the Leopold King Apartments. Brent used a fake identity to hide this past, but the secret was exposed when Mabel Sainsbury-Sil encountered him on Charlotte Street. This encounter was overheard by Brent's great-niece and also alerted Ambrosio, a spy and blackmailer, who identified Brent as a target for extortion.

To silence the blackmailer, Brent orchestrated a series of murders. Poirot details how Brent killed the dentist, Molly, and used an accomplice to move the body. Brent then impersonated a dentist to treat Ambrosio, killing him by injecting a lethal dose of procaine and adrenaline. To cover his tracks, Brent forged patient records for Mrs. Chattam and Miss Sainsbury West, wiped fingerprints, and staged the crime scene. He also attempted to frame Frank Carter by having an accomplice arrange for Carter to work as a gardener, intending to discredit his testimony.

Furthermore, Brent attempted to divert the police by ensuring the body of Mrs. Chattam was found, causing authorities to search for the non-existent Mabel Sainsbury-Sil instead. He also tried to manipulate Poirot by having his wife, a skilled actress, impersonate others over the telephone. Finally, Brent attempted to plant a loaded gun in a laurel bush at Lovecourt to frame a man trimming the bushes, seeking to link the weapon to the death of Muri.
